chartRenderer.empty: No data available for this chart.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Current macro state, near-term risks, and the scenario tests to run next.

scenarioLab.title: Scenario Lab
scenarioLab.description: Build policy scenarios by adjusting assumptions, then review impacts and interpretation in one analysis workspace.

comparison.title: Comparison
comparison.description: Compare baseline and alternative scenarios side by side to surface trade-offs and decision framing.

modelExplorer.title: Model Explorer
modelExplorer.description: Inspect the mechanics behind every output. Honest about what each model does — and does not — do.
modelExplorer.catalogHint: Select one model to inspect technical assumptions and caveats.
modelExplorer.emptyState: No model information is available in this workspace.

empty.assumptions: No assumptions are documented for this model.

briefing.description: Headline values, the current nowcast, and the scenario checks to run next.

artifactBrief.summary: Growth is above the current-quarter nowcast path, while inflation and the external channel remain the main watch points.

dfmContributionDetail.title: Latest indicator contribution slice
dfmContributionDetail.description: Top contributors by absolute contribution, with Industry YoY and Wholesale Trade Growth pinned when needed.

modelTabs.title: Analysis tabs
modelTabs.description: Run each active model in one workspace while keeping its results clearly separated.
modelTabs.plannedDescription: These analysis lanes are shown for orientation only and do not run yet.

subtitle.cgeReformShock: General equilibrium reform analysis, not active

io.title: I-O Sector Shock
io.description: This tab runs final-demand sector transmission analysis with the I-O data layer.

items.inputs: Inputs: demand bucket, amount in billion UZS, distribution mode, and optional sector selector.
items.outputs: Outputs: total-resource requirement, value-added, employment effects, top affected sectors, and linkage classes.
items.boundary: Boundary: sector transmission evidence only; not a macro forecast or general-equilibrium result.

pe.title: Tariff Incidence
pe.description: Direct tariff-incidence analysis from the PE public data file.

items.inputs: Inputs: tariff cut, HS section, partner or regime scope, and section elasticity set.
items.outputs: Outputs: direct trade creation, trade diversion, welfare, revenue, and affected HS sections.
items.boundary: Boundary: direct trade-channel evidence only until linked with I-O, CGE, or synthesis analysis.

cge.title: CGE Reform Shock
cge.description: Economy-wide reform analysis is not active yet.

items.inputs: Expected inputs: productivity, tax, tariff, energy, labor, capital, and closure assumptions.
items.outputs: Expected outputs: welfare, real GDP, sector reallocation, prices, and distributional indicators where supported.
items.boundary: Boundary: general-equilibrium results require a calibrated SAM and model-owner approval before activation.

fpp.title: FPP Fiscal Path
fpp.description: Fiscal path analysis is not active yet.

items.inputs: Expected inputs: revenue, expenditure, financing, debt stock, interest-rate, and growth assumptions.
items.outputs: Expected outputs: deficit, debt path, financing gap, fiscal buffers, and risk flags.
items.boundary: Integration boundary: fiscal sustainability evidence only; it will not backfill QPM, I-O, PE, or CGE outputs without reconciliation.

saved.title: Saved Runs
saved.description: {{count}} saved run(s) are available in this browser session.

items.macro: QPM macro runs keep their existing saved scenario shape.
items.io: I-O runs will be saved as sector-shock outputs after the analytics MVP is implemented.
items.compare: Comparison will read saved outputs and render each model type in its own block.

synthesis.title: Synthesis
synthesis.description: Cross-model reconciliation is not active yet.

items.chain: Target chain: PE tariff incidence -> I-O sector propagation -> CGE reallocation -> FPP fiscal path.
items.layers: QPM and DFM enter as macro consistency and nowcast layers.
items.reconciliation: The synthesis surface will show disagreements, confidence flags, and unresolved assumptions.

ioShock.title: I-O Sector Shock
ioShock.description: Run a final-demand shock through the I-O data layer and inspect sector total-resource and value-added transmission.
ioShock.unavailable: I-O analytics data is unavailable. Existing Scenario Lab behavior is unchanged.
ioShock.policyShockTypeHint: Use cases choose the demand bucket and allocation.
ioShock.sectorHint: Single-sector shocks route the final-demand vector to one of {{sectorCount}}.
ioShock.boundary: Scope: sector accounting only. This is not a macro, price, or employment forecast.
ioShock.savedDescription: Saved I-O sector shock run for sector transmission analysis.
ioShock.sourceLabelNote: Sector labels use the original source names and are not translated here.

claimLabels.gdpContribution: I-O value-added accounting contribution, not macro forecast
claimLabels.employment: Linear employment-intensity estimate, not labor-market forecast

whatThisMeans.title: What this means
whatThisMeans.body: This demand shock produces {{output}} of total-resource requirement and {{valueAdded}} of value-added accounting contribution. Employment is shown as {{employment}} estimated positions from fixed sector intensities.

kpis.multiplierNote: Total resources per 1 UZS final-demand shock

importContent.title: Domestic and import content
importContent.body: Estimated with each sector's source import share. This is an I-O accounting split, not a trade forecast.

concentration.title: Where the effect concentrates
concentration.subtitle: Top sectors by total-resource response. Value-added and employment are shown beside each row.

sensitivity.title: Advanced robustness checks
sensitivity.subtitle: Compare allocation and assumption choices against the base run.

allocation-final-demand.label: Selected final-demand shares
allocation-final-demand.assumption: Uses the selected final-demand bucket as allocation weights.

allocation-output.label: Sector resource shares
allocation-output.assumption: Allocates the same shock by baseline total-resource shares.

allocation-sector.label: One selected sector
allocation-sector.assumption: Assigns the shock to one selected sector.

fx-low.label: FX conversion low
fx-low.assumption: -5% UZS/USD conversion assumption when USD input is active.

fx-high.label: FX conversion high
fx-high.assumption: +5% UZS/USD conversion assumption when USD input is active.

sensitivity.note: Sensitivity rows are deterministic re-runs of the same static I-O calculation; they do not add prices, substitution, fiscal feedback, or general-equilibrium effects.

interpretation.title: Interpretation
interpretation.exposureBody: {{sector}} accounts for {{share}}% of the total-resource effect.
interpretation.noExposure: No sector concentration is available for this shock.
interpretation.boundaryBody: Read this as sector transmission evidence. It is not a price, inflation, or macro forecast.
interpretation.sensitivityBody: Use advanced checks to see whether the sector story changes under allocation, jobs, import, or FX assumptions.
interpretation.nextUseBody: Use this as sector evidence. PE, CGE, or macro links need a separate model handoff before making broader claims.

dataChecks.title: Data quality checks
dataChecks.note: These checks test data-file structure and accounting identities. They do not validate behavioral responses or forecast accuracy.

peShock.title: Tariff Incidence
peShock.description: Test a tariff change by product scope, partner, or trade regime, then inspect direct PE effects before any macro or sector linkage.
peShock.unavailable: PE trade data is unavailable. Existing Scenario Lab behavior is unchanged.
peShock.boundary: Direct tariff-incidence only. These values are not GDP, inflation, I-O, CGE, or fiscal-path effects.
peShock.partnerBoundary: Partner and regime filters use import-share scaling because product-partner tariff matrices are not in the public data file.
peShock.topSections: Largest direct effects by HS section
peShock.saveRunHint: Saved PE runs appear as separate trade-incidence evidence in Comparison.
peShock.savedDescription: Saved PE trade-shock run for direct tariff-incidence analysis.
peShock.sourceLabelNote: Product labels follow the source HS section names. Source: {{source}}.

setup.title: Policy setup
setup.description: Set the tariff direction and choose product, partner, or regime scope.

decision.title: Direct tariff effect
decision.lead: If tariffs are {{direction}} by {{change}} for {{scope}} ({{partnerScope}}), the direct PE result is:
decision.body: A {{change}} tariff change for {{scope}} ({{partnerScope}}) gives {{tradeEffect}} of direct trade effect, {{welfare}} of welfare change, and {{revenue}} of tariff-revenue change.

tradeoff.importBase: Effect relative to selected import base

whatThisMeans.title: What this means
whatThisMeans.body: This tariff cut produces {{tradeEffect}} of direct trade effect, {{welfare}} of welfare effect, and {{revenue}} of tariff-revenue change before macro or sector-propagation effects.

interpretation.title: Interpretation
interpretation.cutEffect: A tariff cut raises direct import demand and consumer welfare, but lowers tariff revenue.
interpretation.increaseEffect: A tariff increase lowers direct import demand and consumer welfare, but raises tariff revenue.
interpretation.exposure: Largest direct effect is concentrated in {{section}}.
interpretation.fiscal: Tariff revenue changes by {{revenue}}; this is an accounting effect, not a fiscal path.
interpretation.next: Save this run, then link it with I-O or CGE before making macro claims.

methodNote.title: Use as
methodNote.direct: Direct, linear PE incidence estimate only.
methodNote.filters: Partner and regime filters are import-share approximations; the public data file has no product-partner tariff matrix.

sensitivity.title: Elasticity sensitivity
sensitivity.description: Shows how the direct trade and welfare effects move if import-demand elasticities are lower or higher than the baseline.

available.label: Available
available.value: HS-section imports, section elasticities, partner import shares.

approximate.label: Approximate
approximate.value: Partner and regime results are import-share scaled.

missing.label: Needed for PE v2
missing.value: Product-partner tariff matrix, HS drill-down, household/firm incidence.

peShock.copyUnavailable: Copy is unavailable in this browser.
peShock.copyNote: PE tariff incidence: {{change}} {{direction}} for {{scope}} ({{partnerScope}}). Direct trade effect {{tradeEffect}}; consumer welfare {{welfare}}; tariff revenue {{revenue}}. Direct PE result only.

savedRuns.title: Saved Runs
savedRuns.description: {{count}} saved run(s) are available in this browser session.
savedRuns.empty: Saved macro, I-O, and PE runs will appear here after you save them.
savedRuns.ioBoundary: Sector transmission evidence only; this is not a macro forecast or general-equilibrium result.

filteredEmpty.all: No saved runs match this filter.

fields.ioSourceCoverage: I-O source tables and public data snapshot

assumptions.title: Assumptions
assumptions.description: Define policy and shock assumptions in plain language.
assumptions.qpmCoreDescription: Direct QPM controls: policy-rate shock, exchange-rate/risk-premium shock, and external-demand shock.
assumptions.linkedDescription: Fiscal spending, tariffs, commodity prices, and remittances are simple scenario mappings around the QPM path, not full endogenous QPM blocks.

policy_rate_change.label: Policy rate change
policy_rate_change.description: Change in policy rate relative to baseline stance.

exchange_rate_change.label: Exchange-rate depreciation
exchange_rate_change.description: Additional depreciation relative to baseline path.

export_demand_change.label: Foreign output gap shock
export_demand_change.description: Foreign output-gap shock entering the QPM IS curve through b3 * gap*_t.

remittance_change.label: Remittance shock
remittance_change.description: Change in remittance inflows against baseline.

commodity_price_change.label: Commodity price shock
commodity_price_change.description: External commodity-price move affecting import bill and inflation.

gov_spending_change.label: Government spending
gov_spending_change.description: Change in government spending relative to baseline.

tax_revenue_change.label: Tax revenue effort
tax_revenue_change.description: Change in tax revenue effort relative to baseline.

tariff_change.label: Import tariff change
tariff_change.description: Change in import tariff setting relative to baseline.

risk_premium_shock.label: Risk premium shock
risk_premium_shock.description: External financing-risk shock affecting policy and activity.

assumptions.emptyCategory: No assumptions available in this category.
assumptions.emptyAdvanced: No advanced assumptions are currently configured.

results.title: Results
results.description: Review headline effects and transmission paths for the current assumptions.
results.impulseResponseCaption: Canonical QPM calculation: each panel shows a deviation from baseline over 12 quarters. The dashed zero line means no scenario effect.

impulsePanels.subtitle: Deviation from baseline; zero line marks no effect.

gdp_gap.title: GDP gap
gdp_gap.takeaway: Positive values indicate demand running above the baseline path.

inflation.title: Inflation
inflation.takeaway: Positive values indicate inflation above the baseline path.

policy_rate.title: Policy rate
policy_rate.takeaway: Positive values indicate a higher policy-rate path than baseline.

decision.title: Effect by {{period}}
decision.lead: If “{{scenarioName}}” is applied, the QPM result is shown first as the change versus baseline.
decision.note: The baseline path is anchored to the latest Overview snapshot. Scenario values add QPM shock effects around that baseline. External and fiscal tabs are accounting views.

explanations.headlineImpact: Shows how the selected scenario deviates from the baseline across 12 quarters; values are percentage-point deviations from the canonical QPM calculation.
explanations.macroPath: Shows the scenario effect as scenario minus baseline. Endpoint cards keep the level comparison.
explanations.externalBalance: Shows the current-account effect as scenario minus baseline. This is an accounting/proxy view, not an endogenous QPM external-sector block.
explanations.fiscalEffects: Shows the fiscal effect as scenario minus baseline. This is an accounting/proxy view, not an endogenous QPM fiscal block.

effectChart.title: {{metric}} effect vs baseline
effectChart.subtitle: Scenario minus baseline; zero line means no scenario effect.
effectChart.takeaway: Read this as the scenario effect over time, not as a standalone forecast level.

baselineSource.title: Latest Overview snapshot
baselineSource.summary: Baseline path uses the latest Overview snapshot; source details are kept here.

activeShocks.title: Active shocks
activeShocks.none: No active shocks; this is a baseline-style reference run.

pathDeltas.noMaterialDifference: No material difference from the baseline path under this scenario.

interpretation.title: Interpretation
interpretation.description: Translate model outputs into decision language.

items.gdpGrowthDelta: GDP growth is {{delta}} pp versus baseline by 2026 Q4.
items.inflationNoShock: Inflation is {{delta}} pp versus baseline; no additional price shock channel is selected.
items.inflationDrivers: Inflation is {{delta}} pp versus baseline; active price channels: {{drivers}}.
items.balanceDrivers: External and fiscal balances move through {{drivers}}.
items.balanceNearBaseline: External and fiscal balances stay near baseline because remittance, trade, spending, and revenue settings are unchanged.
items.domesticDemandSequence: Domestic demand and price channels respond first, then external and fiscal balances adjust.
items.passThroughRisk: Pass-through may be stronger than assumed when exchange-rate shocks are persistent.
items.fiscalExternalRisk: Fiscal and external shocks can amplify each other in downside cases.
items.sequencePolicy: Sequence monetary and fiscal decisions to avoid conflicting signals.
items.targetedMitigation: Use targeted mitigation if downside scenarios widen the growth-inflation trade-off.

suggestedNext.compare_with_baseline_and_tight_money: Compare with baseline and tight money

aiAttribution.title: AI-assisted · Unreviewed draft
aiAttribution.body: This interpretation was drafted from structured simulation outputs using the {{engine}}. Human review is required before citing externally.

assisted.title: AI-assisted · Unreviewed draft
assisted.body: AI-assisted draft. Generated by model output, not yet reviewed by CERR analytical staff. Do not cite, export, or share externally.

reviewed.title: AI-assisted · Reviewed {{reviewed_at}}
reviewed.body: AI-assisted narrative, reviewed by {{reviewer_name}} on {{review_date}}. Cleared for internal use and citation.
